title: Repo Index — Bounded On-Demand Locator
goal: Add a bounded on-demand /repo-index <scope> command under the read-only explore agent that returns a current, source-citing locator map (max ~40 lines / ~1,500 tokens), with a proof gate comparing localization turns vs the explore/zoom-out baseline; keep only if it measurably reduces localization turns without reducing source reads.

success_criteria.check: Command exists and is bounded (scope arg, ~40-line / ~1,500-token budget, locator note)
Read-only + source-citing (runs under explore; every citation is a real path:line)
success_criteria.verify: rg -n "agent: explore" .opencode/command/repo-index.md && run the command on a scope and confirm each citation resolves

success_criteria.check: No tracked index file, no injection change, no verify change
Proof gate runs (recorded comparison of localization turns vs baseline on >=3 real tasks incl. 1 consumer)
Keep/delete decision recorded with evidence in progress.md + MEMORY.md
No drift introduced (no committed snapshot; if deleted, no residual files)
success_criteria.verify: git status --porcelain shows only intended changes

tasks.title: Bounded /repo-index <scope> command under explore agent
tasks.end_state: The /repo-index command exists, runs read-only under explore, takes a scope arg, enforces a ~40-line / ~1,500-token budget, cites path:line, and includes a locator-only note. No tracked file, no injection, no verify change.
tasks.verify: test -f .opencode/command/repo-index.md && rg -n "agent: explore|scope|40|1,500|locator" .opencode/command/repo-index.md && test ! -e .opencode/repo-index.md && git diff --exit-code .opencode/tool/verify.sh .opencode/opencode.json

tasks.title: Proof gate on real tasks (>=3 tasks incl. 1 consumer)
tasks.end_state: A recorded comparison of localization turns and wrong-file starts vs the explore/zoom-out baseline on >=3 representative tasks, with a keep/delete recommendation in progress.md.

tasks.title: Keep/delete decision + closeout
tasks.end_state: Apply stop conditions. If kept: document in MEMORY.md + roadmap.md. If deleted: document the negative result. Update AGENTS.md/README.md to match final state. Mark artifact Complete. Remove .active.

open_questions.question: What scopes are most useful? (subsystem, symbol, task-type, file-pattern)
open_questions.resolution: Resolve during Task 2 (proof gate reveals which scopes the agent requests)

open_questions.question: What's the proof-gate task set? (which 3+ tasks, which consumer project)
open_questions.resolution: Resolve during Task 2 (user picks representative tasks)

open_questions.question: Should explore.md get a one-line pointer to /repo-index?
open_questions.resolution: Resolve during Task 1 (surgical; only if it reduces discovery friction)

stop_conditions: AGENTS.md + one targeted search already identifies the work site within the existing explore budget (<=3 calls/symbol)
No repeated wrong-file starts or localization bottleneck has been observed
The output mostly repeats AGENTS.md, README, or .template-manifest.json
It cannot stay bounded without omitting the information users actually need
It helps only this template and not at least one real consumer project
Agents still repeat the same broad searches after reading it (no net reduction)
Correctness requires behavioral summaries instead of source locators
The design requires auto-injection, embeddings, a database, watchers, or a committed generated snapshot
non_goals: A new verify.sh check or structural-check rule
A Bun TS generator tool, cache, watcher, database, embeddings, or incremental hashing infra
Auto-injection of any index into the system prompt
Behavioral summaries that replace source for edits
